provenance.source_corpus: Post-fire regeneration of endangered limber pine (Pinus flexilis) at the northern extent of its range

nodes.name: extensive prescribed burns and stand-replacing wildfire events

source_spans.text: Our findings suggest that extensive prescribed burns and stand-replacing wildfire events at the northern extent of limber pine’s range may have limited seedling regeneration in the immediate post-fire period.

Our findings suggest that extensive prescribed burns and stand-replacing wildfire events at the northern extent of limber pine’s range may have limited seedling regeneration in the immediate post-fire period.
Akaike information criterion (AIC) identified distance to a seed source and absence of canopy cover as hurdles for seedling establishment in the burn.
Akaike information criterion (AIC) identified distance to a seed source and absence of canopy cover as hurdles for seedling establishment in the burn.
substrate variables, including less prevalent bare mineral soil, more rocky cover, and finer soil textures, take on an important role in driving abundance.
In both Upper Saskatchewan and Dogrib, fire appeared to boost regeneration of the dominant tree species in the region (lodgepole pine and white spruce, respectively)...

nodes.name: regeneration of the dominant tree species

source_spans.text: In both Upper Saskatchewan and Dogrib, fire appeared to boost regeneration of the dominant tree species in the region (lodgepole pine and white spruce, respectively)...

nodes.name: basal area of alive limber pine

source_spans.text: In the seed model, basal area of alive limber pine was retained and found to have a positive association with seedling abundance
In the seed model, basal area of alive limber pine was retained and found to have a positive association with seedling abundance
